evals.prompt: Write me an obsidian CLI script for Android/Termux that uses Advanced URI for write actions (open, append, prepend, daily, command, search) and local ripgrep/find for read queries (tags, tag, links, backlinks, file, files, folder, folders, outline, move). My vault is at ~/storage/shared/Documents/Obsidian and vault name is MyVault.
evals.expected_output: A complete bash script with all commands implemented, VAULT and VAULT_PATH config variables at top, and a help/usage case for unknown commands.

evals.prompt: I want to search for all files tagged with #todo in my obsidian vault, show me which files have it, and count the total occurrences. Also show me all unique tags across my entire vault sorted by frequency.
evals.expected_output: Script should call 'obsidian tag name="todo" verbose' for the search, and 'obsidian tags counts sort=count' for global tag frequencies. Output includes file paths and counts.

evals.prompt: Show me the outline/heading tree for my note at path 'Projects/Active.md', how many links total are in it, and what files link to it (backlinks). Also list all PDF files in the Attachments folder.
evals.expected_output: Three commands: 'obsidian outline path="Projects/Active.md"' (tree format with line numbers), 'obsidian links path="Projects/Active.md" total' (count), 'obsidian backlinks path="Projects/Active.md"' (file list), and 'obsidian files folder="Attachments" ext=".pdf"'.
